text: : قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي رَجُلٍ تَكَفَّلَ بِنَفْسِ رَجُلٍ أَنْ يُحْبَسَ وَ قَالَ لَهُ "اُطْلُبْ صَاحِبَكَ" وَ قَضَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "أَنَّهُ لاَ كَفَالَةَ فِي حَدٍّ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3400 - Sa'd ibn Tarif narrated from Al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah:
"Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled in the case of a man who guaranteed the presence of another man that he should be detained, and Imam (as) said to him, "Seek your companion."
And Imam (as) ruled: 'There is no guarantee (kafalah) in matters involving prescribed punishments (hudud).'"
